title: Chicken Karahi

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Karahi Recipe: Easy Pakistani Dish
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Pakistani cooking with our authentic Chicken Karahi recipe. Learn how to make this flavorful, easy chicken dish at home with step-by-step instructions.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chicken Karahi recipe served in a traditional karahi dish, garnished with ginger and cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich flavors of Pakistan with this easy-to-follow Chicken Karahi recipe, perfect for any occasion.

description: Chicken Karahi is a quintessential Pakistani dish, celebrated for its robust, spicy tomato-based gravy and succulent chicken pieces. Traditionally prepared in a heavy-bottomed wok called a karahi, it features a vibrant blend of fresh ginger, garlic, and green chilies. This recipe promises an authentic taste of Pakistan, delivering a flavorful and aromatic meal perfect for any occasion.

ingredients.notes: slit lengthwise, more or less to taste

instructions.title: Brown the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat cooking oil in a large karahi or wok over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces and fry until they are lightly browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. This seals in the juices and develops flavor.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics
instructions.text: Stir in the ginger garlic paste and continue to cook for another 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the paste, stirring constantly.

instructions.title: Introduce Tomatoes and Spices
instructions.text: Add the chopped or pureed tomatoes to the karahi. Immediately follow with cumin powder, coriander powder, red chili powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Mix well to combine all ingredients.

instructions.title: Cook Down the Masala
instructions.text: Cover the karahi and cook on medium-low heat for 15-20 minutes, or until the tomatoes have softened completely and the oil begins to separate from the masala. Mash the tomatoes occasionally with the back of your spoon to create a smooth gravy. If using, stir in the whisked yogurt now and cook for another 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Green Chilies and Simmer
instructions.text: Stir in the slit green chilies and black pepper powder. Cook uncovered for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the gravy to thicken to your desired consistency. Taste and adjust salt if needed.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Finally, sprinkle with garam masala powder, julienned fresh ginger, and chopped fresh coriander. Give it a gentle mix or simply let the garnish sit on top. Serve hot with naan, roti, or rice.

tips: Always use a heavy-bottomed pan or traditional karahi for even cooking and better flavor development, preventing sticking.
For a richer color and deeper flavor, use fresh, ripe tomatoes. If tomatoes are not very red, a small amount of tomato paste can be added with the spices.
Adjust the amount of green chilies and red chili powder according to your spice preference. For an extra kick, add a few more green chilies towards the end of cooking.
article: Chicken Karahi is a beloved Pakistani Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
